session.user:

1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_plan_label.py` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_plan_labels.py` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_pr.py` 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_prs.py` 5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/create_impl_context_from_plan.py`
This includes: - Imports - Parameter names - Variable names and attribute accesses - Type annotations - Comments
DO NOT rename: - External API field names - User-facing string messages
Read each file first, then apply all renames using the Edit tool.

1→"""Backend-aware label addition for plans. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec add-plan-label <pr-number> --label <label> 5→ 6→Output: 7→ JSON with {success, pr_number, label} 8→ 9→Exit Codes: 10→ 0: Success - label added 11→ 1: Error - plan not found or API error 12→ 2: Usage error - missing required --label flag 13→""" 14→ 15→import json 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_plan_backend, require_repo_root 20→ 21→ 22→@click.command(name="add-plan-label") 23→@click.argument("pr_number", type=int) 24→@click.option( 25→ "--label", 26→ required=True, 27→ help="Label to add to the PR", 28→) 29→@click.pass_context 30→def add_plan_label(
... [39 lines omitted] ...
44→ except RuntimeError as e: 49→ "error": f"Failed to add label to plan #{pr_number}: {e}",

1→"""Batch add labels to multiple plans. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ echo '[{"pr_number": 42, "label": "erk-learn"}]' | erk exec add-plan-labels 5→ 6→Input: 7→ JSON array from stdin, each item: {"pr_number": int, "label": str} 8→ 9→Output: 10→ JSON with batch results 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Always (even on error, to support batch contract) 14→ 1: Context not initialized 15→""" 16→ 17→from __future__ import annotations 18→ 19→import json 20→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 21→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypedDict, cast 22→ 23→import click 24→ 25→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_plan_backend, require_repo_root 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from typing import Any 29→ 30→
... [162 lines omitted] ...
47→class BatchLabelError: 48→ """Error response for batch label addition.""" 51→ error_type: str 55→def _validate_batch_input(data: object) -> list[PlanLabelItem] | BatchLabelError: 62→ List of validated items, or BatchLabelError on validation failure 65→ return BatchLabelError( 67→ error_type="invalid-input", 74→ return BatchLabelError( 76→ error_type="invalid-input", 83→ return BatchLabelError( 85→ error_type="invalid-input", 91→ return BatchLabelError( 93→ error_type="invalid-input", 98→ return BatchLabelError( 100→ error_type="invalid-input", 106→ return BatchLabelError( 108→ error_type="invalid-input", 137→ except json.JSONDecodeError as e: 138→ result = BatchLabelError( 140→ error_type="invalid-json", 141→ message=f"Failed to parse JSON: {e}", 148→ if isinstance(validated, BatchLabelError): 162→ except RuntimeError as e: 167→ "error": f"Failed to add label: {e}",

1→"""Close a plan with a comment. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec close-pr <PR_NUMBER> --comment "Closing because..." 5→ 6→Output: 7→ JSON with {success, pr_number, comment_id} 8→ 9→Exit Codes: 10→ 0: Success - plan closed with comment 11→ 1: Error - plan not found or API error 12→""" 13→ 14→import json 15→ 16→import click 17→ 18→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 19→ require_plan_backend, 20→ require_repo_root, 21→) 22→ 23→ 24→@click.command(name="close-pr") 25→@click.argument("pr_number", type=int) 26→@click.option( 27→ "--comment", 28→ required=True, 29→ help="Comment body to add before closing", 30→)
... [56 lines omitted] ...
46→ except RuntimeError as e: 51→ "error": f"Failed to add comment to PR #{pr_number}: {e}", 60→ except RuntimeError as e: 65→ "error": f"Failed to close PR #{pr_number}: {e}",

1→"""Batch close multiple plan PRs with comments. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ echo '[{"pr_number": 42, "comment": "Superseded"}]' | erk exec close-prs 5→ 6→Input: 7→ JSON array from stdin, each item: {"pr_number": int, "comment": str} 8→ 9→Output: 10→ JSON with batch results 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Always (even on error, to support batch contract) 14→ 1: Context not initialized 15→""" 16→ 17→from __future__ import annotations 18→ 19→import json 20→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 21→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypedDict, cast 22→ 23→import click 24→ 25→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_plan_backend, require_repo_root 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from typing import Any 29→ 30→
... [175 lines omitted] ...
47→class BatchCloseError: 48→ """Error response for batch close.""" 51→ error_type: str 55→def _validate_batch_input(data: object) -> list[PlanCloseItem] | BatchCloseError: 62→ List of validated items, or BatchCloseError on validation failure 65→ return BatchCloseError( 67→ error_type="invalid-input", 74→ return BatchCloseError( 76→ error_type="invalid-input", 83→ return BatchCloseError( 85→ error_type="invalid-input", 91→ return BatchCloseError( 93→ error_type="invalid-input", 98→ return BatchCloseError( 100→ error_type="invalid-input", 106→ return BatchCloseError( 108→ error_type="invalid-input", 137→ except json.JSONDecodeError as e: 138→ result = BatchCloseError( 140→ error_type="invalid-json", 141→ message=f"Failed to parse JSON: {e}", 148→ if isinstance(validated, BatchCloseError): 162→ except RuntimeError as e: 167→ "error": f"Failed to add comment: {e}", 174→ except RuntimeError as e: 179→ "error": f"Failed to close PR: {e}",

1→"""Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from plan content. 2→ 3→This exec command fetches a plan via ManagedPrBackend and creates the .erk/impl-context/ 4→folder structure, providing a testable alternative to inline workflow scripts. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan <plan-id> 8→ 9→Output: 10→ Structured JSON output with success status and folder details 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (.erk/impl-context/ folder created) 14→ 1: Error (plan not found, fetch failed, folder creation failed) 15→ 16→Examples: 17→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 1028 18→ {"success": true, "impl_context_path": "/path/to/.erk/impl-context", "pr_number": 1028} 19→ 20→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 999 21→ {"success": false, "error": "plan_not_found", "message": "..."} 22→""" 23→ 24→import json 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 29→ require_plan_backend, 30→ require_repo_root,
... [67 lines omitted] ...
61→ error_output = { 63→ "error": "plan_not_found", 67→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_output), err=True)
